feedback.text: I believe games are not just mindless fun - they can be works of art, stories, and shared experiences that enrich our lives. Yet too often, developers take away the games weve paid for by shutting down servers or removing access, leaving us with nothing. Thats why I hope the proposed Digital Fairness Act will require developers to leave games in a playable, standalone state - no servers, addons, or extra costs needed - so we can still enjoy them even after official support ends. Just as the EUs Copyright in the Digital Single Market Directive ensures old books stay available for future generations, lets give games the same treatment.
